reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully encodes Munkres Exercise 19.6a. The product space $\prod X_\alpha$ is encoded as `Πa, f a` with the canonical `Pi.topologicalSpace` instance inferred from `[Πa, TopologicalSpace (f a)]`, which is precisely the product (Tychonoff) topology. The sequence is `x : ℕ → Πa, f a`, its limit candidate is `y`, and `Tendsto x atTop (𝓝 y)` is sequence convergence in the product. The right side `∀ i, Tendsto (λ j => (x j) i) atTop (𝓝 (y i))` is exactly "for each α, $\pi_\alpha(\mathbf{x}_j) \to \pi_\alpha(\mathbf{x})$". The biconditional matches "if and only if". This is essentially `tendsto_pi_nhds` specialized to `atTop`, so the formalization is logically equivalent to the informal claim with no hidden weakening or strengthening.
